content.claim: The 6- and 8-year gimnázium tracks were established in 1989–1990 as a post-regime-change cross-ideological coalition outcome, supported by church interests seeking restoration of pre-communist traditions and by liberals favoring decentralization and local control over school structure.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: The 2010 study is cited indirectly through journalistic reporting rather than direct academic access
The inference linking church interests and conservative ideology to track creation is analytical rather than explicitly documented in the cited source
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
